name: Involtini Di Pollo (Stuffed Chicken Breasts)
recipeIngredient: 1 large chicken breast, boneless, skinless, halved
1 cup Italian bread crumbs, crust removed
3 green onions, white parts and 1 inch of green parts thinly sliced
1 teaspoon fresh sage or ½ tsp. dried sage
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Place each chicken breast half between two sheets of waxed paper.
3. Press down and then push out from the center of each breast half, flattening it evenly. Do not over pound the breasts as they may break apart.
4. To make the stuffing: Soak bread in stock for 2-3 minutes. In a skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add green onions and sauté until softened but not browned about 3 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Squeeze excess stock from bread; discard the stock. Add bread to bowl with sage, parsley, salt, and pepper. Season well.
6. Place a slice of ham on each flattened chicken breast half. Divide stuffing between breasts, spreading over the ham. Roll up from a narrow end, leaving opposite end uncovered and slightly overlapping.
7. Tuck in any stuffing that may have leaked out; ham prevents leakage. Tie rolls closed with kitchen string or toothpicks.
8. In a skillet, melt butter with olive oil over medium heat. Dredge chicken rolls in flour, then add to hot pan without cooking for more than 5 minutes total. Avoid overcooking the delicate breast meat as it will become tough.
9. Transfer sauteed rolls to a casserole dish large enough to accommodate them without touching. Set skillet aside for deglazing later. Pour stock over breasts and cover tightly, placing in preheated oven.
10. Bake until cooked through but still juicy, 20-25 minutes. Avoid cooking more than five minutes as it will dry out the meat.
11. Remove rolls from oven, string, and toothpicks. Let stand for 5-10 minutes.
12. Heat pan drippings in skillet, adding stock from casserole and simmering until alcohol evaporates. Season with salt.
13. Cut stuffed breasts crosswise into slices, arranging on serving platter. Pour hot pan juices over them. Serve warm or hot.
